scope: Bounded implementation plan only. Patch existing files, services, workflows, and tests where practical. Preserve current architecture, operations, deterministic parsing, violation search, public API behavior unless fail-closed behavior is required, and the current Vitest/script validation framework.

forbiddenCompletionEvidence: simulated proof represented as production proof
completionRule.productionReadyClaimRule: If a production-ready claim cannot be automated, output CERTIFYING:false.

findings.title: Evidence Ledger Allows Caller-Supplied Hashes, Updates, And Deletes
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Existing evidence_event table, packet/tradeline ownership checks, hashChain verification semantics, evidence list response shape, packet evidence consumers, and bureau communication transaction pattern.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Remove or ignore caller-supplied hash fields from normal evidence create behavior.
Add a thin append-only evidence ledger helper that computes previousHash and currentHash server-side inside a transaction.
Convert update/delete routes into correction or retraction event writers while preserving route compatibility where possible.
Keep any raw mutation behind admin-only audited remediation with a required reason.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Client hash fields cannot set persisted hashes.
Concurrent append simulation creates a valid ordered chain.
Update/delete append correction or retraction events without mutating original rows.
verifyChain succeeds for valid chains and fails for tampering.
Non-owner evidence attempts remain denied.
commandsToRun: pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts tests/unit/evidence-event-ledger.spec.ts tests/api/evidence-privacy-endpoint.spec.ts
doneCriteria: Normal evidence writes are append-only and server-hashed.
Existing consumers can still list and use evidence events.
Automated tests prove raw updates/deletes no longer rewrite evidence truth.
CERTIFYING:false remains unless current promotion evidence includes the ledger proof.
regressionRisks: Packet generation may depend on prior evidence row shape.
Existing UI update/delete affordances may expect mutation semantics.
Chain ordering can race without a real transaction boundary.

findings.title: Artifact Storage Is Not Certifiably Durable Across Deploys Or Rollbacks
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Existing local: storage URLs, object names, report artifact storage, packet PDF storage, document storage reads, and object-storage fallback behavior.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Add a deploy preflight that fails closed unless storage is object-backed or the local root is an explicit persistent mount.
Add a synthetic sentinel write/read/delete proof that never touches consumer artifacts.
Add static workflow validation proving the preflight runs before container replacement.
Document the storage contract without exposing environment values.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Production-like local ephemeral path is rejected.
Explicit mounted root passes with mocked sentinel IO.
Object-storage mode passes without live provider calls.
Deploy workflow static test proves preflight precedes container replacement.
commandsToRun: pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts tests/unit/storage-durability-preflight.spec.ts tests/unit/deploy-production-workflow.spec.ts tests/unit/deploy-staging-workflow.spec.ts tests/unit/report-artifact-storage.spec.ts tests/unit/evidence-attachment-storage.spec.ts tests/unit/packet-pdf-cache.spec.ts
doneCriteria: Production-like deploys fail before replacement when artifact storage durability cannot be proven automatically.
Existing local: artifacts remain readable.
No live provider calls are introduced.
Evidence output explicitly states whether storage proof is certifying.
regressionRisks: Misclassifying local development storage as production can block dev workflows.
Compose mount edits can affect deployment paths.
Sentinel cleanup must not delete real artifact prefixes.

groups.title: P1 queue, persistence, deploy, parser, packet blockers

findings.title: Rollback SHA Validation Tests The Workflow Ref, Not The Rollback Target
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Existing workflow dispatch inputs, target SHA output, health checks, production-safe privacy probes, staging smokes, and deploy branch policy.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Resolve TARGET_SHA before any validation job that claims rollback readiness.
Checkout the exact target SHA for validation and evidence generation.
Prove target SHA reachability from the approved branch.
Fail evidence generation when reported target SHA differs from git rev-parse HEAD.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Workflow static test proves rollback dispatch validation uses steps.target.outputs.sha.
Workflow static test proves target reachability is checked.
Deployment parity evidence embeds and validates target SHA.
doneCriteria: Rollback validation and deploy target are the same SHA in automated evidence.
Normal deploys continue to use the triggering SHA.
A mismatched evidence SHA fails closed.
regressionRisks: Workflow permissions or checkout depth can prevent reachability checks.
Evidence scripts may need target SHA plumbing.

findings.title: Deployment Replaces Containers Without Automatic Rollback Or Blue-Green Safety
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Current single-host Docker Compose deployment, health-check URLs, privacy denial probes, and staging/production separation.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Capture previous SHA/container or image before replacement.
Add a failure trap that restores the previous known-good state when post-deploy health fails.
Keep blue-green as optional future work and first implement bounded automatic rollback.
Ensure rollback commands do not expose secrets and do not execute during local tests.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Workflow static test proves previous SHA capture occurs before deploy.
Workflow static test proves failure path calls rollback before workflow exit.
Evidence script test reports rollback capability as non-certifying when missing.
doneCriteria: A failed post-deploy health check has an automated rollback path.
Static workflow tests prove ordering.
Evidence reports prior SHA and rollback readiness without manual deploy testing.
regressionRisks: Rollback can conflict with non-backward-compatible migrations.
Health-check timing changes can cause false rollbacks.

findings.title: Request-Bound Immediate Ingest Processing Reintroduces High-Cost Work Into The HTTP Path
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Durable ingest queue records, idempotency behavior, SSE-compatible status responses, executeIngestPipeline worker path, deterministic parser behavior, and current violation-search behavior.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Limit the HTTP endpoint to validation, enqueue/attach, and status streaming in staging/production.
Move immediate processing behind a non-production flag that fails closed in staging/production.
Use existing queue service metrics for liveness and backpressure.
Preserve upload response contracts except where fail-closed behavior is required.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Production-like endpoint test proves executeIngestPipeline is not called by the request path.
Local/demo flag test proves immediate mode is unavailable unless explicitly non-production.
Duplicate submission still collapses to the active job.
SSE/status output remains compatible for queued/running/retry/dead-letter states.
doneCriteria: Staging/production request path cannot execute parser/OCR/compliance work inline.
Queue idempotency and worker processing still work.
Automated tests prove the boundary.
regressionRisks: Upload UI may rely on immediate completion events.
Existing lifecycle tests may encode current request-bound behavior.
A missing worker in staging must surface as fail-closed status.

findings.title: Ingest Persistence Allows Partial Writes And Replay Drift
findings.subsystemToPreserve: executeIngestPipeline, canonical parser output, report artifact compatibility, replay payload format, evidence indexes, compliance scan outputs, and packet readiness inputs.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Define atomic stage boundaries for parser output, canonical mapping, evidence indexes, compliance results, replay payload, and final status.
Use transactions for promotion from staged output to active report state.
Represent optional non-critical write failures as explicit degraded state with queue/operator visibility.
Keep old records readable and avoid destructive migration.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Failure after parser output does not mark final status complete.
Failure during compliance persistence preserves previous active state.
Replay validator proves persisted truth matches staged canonical output.
Optional degraded writes are visible without changing canonical truth.
doneCriteria: Active report state is promoted only after required stage writes succeed.
Failures leave retryable or degraded state with automated proof.
Deterministic replay remains stable.
regressionRisks: Transaction scope can become too large for heavy parsing outputs.
Existing storage functions may assume best-effort behavior.
Old partial records must remain readable.

findings.title: Compliance Findings Can Be Deleted Before Replacement Inserts Succeed
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Current detector logic, manual/admin finding preservation, active auto-generated finding semantics, packet readiness consumers, and violation-search behavior.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Wrap delete and replacement insert in one transaction.
Prefer insert into a staged replacement set followed by an atomic active-set swap.
On any insert failure, preserve the previous active auto-generated set and record degraded scan state.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Insert failure after delete point preserves previous active findings.
Manual/admin findings remain untouched.
Successful replacement yields exactly the new generated set.
Packet readiness sees old complete set or new complete set, never partial.
doneCriteria: Compliance replacement is all-or-nothing.
Manual/admin records are preserved.
Automated failure injection proves no partial deletion.
regressionRisks: Existing tests may expect insert failures to be swallowed.
Transaction semantics can differ between test DB and production DB.

findings.title: Parser Rule Promotion Can Bypass Regression Gates While Mutating Canonical Truth
findings.subsystemToPreserve: DB-backed parser extraction rules, canonical extractor flow, admin rule promotion workflow, deterministic parser AI-disabled boundary, and current violation-search behavior.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Reject runRegressionGate:false outside test-only contexts unless audited break-glass is explicitly implemented.
Store regression gate evidence metadata with the promoted rule.
Refuse active rule activation without passing gate evidence.
Keep parser logic deterministic and do not introduce AI fallback.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Non-test promotion with runRegressionGate:false is rejected.
Test-only bypass remains isolated from staging/production env.
Active rule activation requires stored passing gate evidence.
Canonical extraction still applies approved active rules deterministically.
doneCriteria: No active parser truth change can bypass automated regression evidence.
Existing approved parser rule behavior remains stable.
Evidence captures rule ID, target cases, command, result, and commit.
regressionRisks: Admin parser tooling may send runRegressionGate explicitly.
Old promoted rules may lack evidence metadata and need compatibility handling.

findings.title: Packet PDF Cache-Miss Timeout Does Not Cancel Underlying Render Work
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Existing packet PDF bytes, content-addressed cache keys, packet PDF evidence events, route responses, and packet readiness behavior.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: If renderer abort is unavailable, keep the cache-miss concurrency slot occupied until the render promise settles.
Emit degraded async-render or retry-after state instead of releasing capacity while work continues.
Keep cache hits fast and unchanged.
Avoid out-of-process rendering until bounded slot behavior is proven insufficient.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Timed-out render promise keeps capacity occupied until settlement.
New cache misses are rejected or queued while timed-out work remains running.
Cache hit behavior bypasses the miss envelope.
Render failure evidence records sanitized failure data.
commandsToRun: pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts tests/unit/packet-pdf-cache.spec.ts tests/api/packet-lifecycle-endpoint.spec.ts tests/api/packet-delivery-status-endpoint.spec.ts
doneCriteria: Timeout cannot create more concurrent render work than the configured envelope.
Existing PDF content and cache-hit behavior remain unchanged.
Automated cache-miss proof reflects cancellation or slot semantics.
regressionRisks: Holding slots longer can increase visible retry-after responses.
Tests must avoid relying on real long PDF renders.

findings.title: Current Queue State Shows Critical Staleness And No Drained-Queue Proof
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Existing durable queue, dry-run default worker, bounded apply guards, remediation actions, append-only queue events, and operator dashboard categories.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Add worker heartbeat and oldest queued age SLO evidence through existing queue metrics.
Add stale queue fail-closed status to readiness evidence.
Add simulated queue-drain proof and staging-safe proof path while separating simulated/staging proof from production certification.
Do not require manual queue draining.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Worker heartbeat events update liveness without processing real jobs.
Dashboard fails when oldest queued age exceeds SLO and no heartbeat exists.
Simulated queue drain proves scoped jobs leave no eligible queued remainder.
Readiness evidence remains CERTIFYING:false when only simulated proof exists.
doneCriteria: Queue liveness is visible and automated.
Stale queue state blocks promotion evidence.
No manual drain is required to close local/staging proof.
regressionRisks: Local queues with old jobs can keep dashboard failing.
Heartbeat records must not be mistaken for processed jobs.

findings.title: SSH Host Key Trust Uses Runtime ssh-keyscan
findings.subsystemToPreserve: SSH-based deployment and existing GitHub environment separation; do not create or print secrets, deploy keys, or host credentials.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Add host key fingerprint verification before adding scanned keys to known_hosts.
Read expected fingerprints from GitHub environment configuration without creating credentials in code.
Fail closed if expected fingerprint configuration is absent in production-like deploys.
Keep local static tests mocked and non-networked.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Workflow static test proves scanned keys are compared to expected fingerprint.
Missing expected fingerprint fails production deploy setup.
Mismatched fingerprint fails before SSH commands.
doneCriteria: Production-like SSH setup cannot trust runtime scan alone.
No secret values are printed.
Evidence reports CERTIFYING:false if host fingerprint configuration cannot be verified automatically.
regressionRisks: GitHub environments must provide expected fingerprints before deploy can pass.
Rotated host keys require controlled config update.

findings.title: Remote Deployment Mutates Working Tree And Staging Lacks Post-Checkout SHA Verification
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Current compose services, remote checkout path, target SHA deploy flow, and staging/production health checks.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Stop copying docker-compose.production.yml over docker-compose.yml.
Call Docker Compose with -f docker-compose.production.yml for production.
Add staging post-checkout git rev-parse HEAD equality verification matching production.
Add static workflow checks for both behaviors.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Production workflow test fails if compose file copy returns.
Staging workflow test proves checked out SHA equals TARGET_SHA.
Deploy parity evidence records compose file mode and SHA verification.
doneCriteria: Remote checkout remains unmutated by compose file copying.
Staging and production both verify deployed SHA.
Existing deploy service names remain unchanged.
regressionRisks: Remote Docker Compose commands may rely on the old copied file name.
Staging parity changes can expose existing remote drift.

findings.title: Bureau Communication Attachment Hashes Base64 Text, Not Decoded Bytes
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Bureau communication ownership checks, attachment storage path, evidence event creation, response classification, obligation/packet updates, and storage metadata.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Replace string hash of fileDataBase64 with sha256HexOfBase64Payload.
Store byte digest and MIME/size metadata in attachment metadata.
Keep old metadata readable.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Equivalent base64 encodings and data URL formatting produce the same byte hash.
Different bytes produce different hashes.
Attachment storage still returns existing metadata shape.
doneCriteria: Attachment evidence hash matches decoded bytes.
Existing bureau communication route behavior remains intact.
Tests prove no live external provider calls.
regressionRisks: Existing records hashed as base64 text will not match new byte digest.
Downstream displays may assume old metadata key names.

findings.title: Migration Governance Remains Partial And Runtime Schema Ensures Mask Drift
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Current non-mutating migration checker, migration ledger convention, runtime ensure compatibility during cutover, and existing schema behavior.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Convert runtime ensure paths into reviewed additive migrations one subsystem per task.
Keep runtime ensure functions in compatibility mode until matching migration proof exists.
Enable hard migration gate only after warning-only paths are closed or explicitly waived with automated evidence.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Checker identifies converted ensure path as migration-backed.
Migration gate fails when unknown runtime schema mutation is present.
Runtime ensure compatibility remains no-op or idempotent after migration.
commandsToRun: pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts tests/unit/migration-checker.spec.ts tests/unit/migration-gate.spec.ts
doneCriteria: Each converted schema path has a reviewed ledger entry and automated proof.
Hard gate is not enabled until automated evidence supports it.
Schema behavior is unchanged except for reviewed additive migrations.
regressionRisks: Duplicating runtime ensure DDL and migrations can cause drift.
A hard gate enabled too early can block deploys.

findings.title: Evidence Hash Chain Semantics Are Inconsistent Across Packet And General Evidence Paths
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Packet generation, packet evidence event content, packet readiness, evidence list behavior, and hash-chain verification API.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Route packet-generated evidence and general evidence through the same append-only ledger helper.
Keep event payloads compatible while standardizing previousHash and currentHash.
Add correction/retraction event types instead of direct mutation.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Packet generation event chains from the same helper as general evidence.
Mixed packet/general evidence sequence verifies with verifyChain.
Existing packet evidence details remain present.
commandsToRun: pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts tests/unit/evidence-event-ledger.spec.ts tests/unit/dispute-packet-evidence-location.spec.ts tests/unit/dispute-packet-template.spec.ts
doneCriteria: All normal evidence producers use one append-only chain path.
Packet evidence is still generated and visible.
Hash-chain proof is automated.
regressionRisks: Packet service may run inside broader transactions and need helper transaction injection.
Existing generated evidence snapshots may need intentional updates.

findings.title: Operational Evidence Is Stale Or Explicitly Non-Certifying In Multiple Places
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Existing production-scale evidence framework, blocker registry, promotion-pack command, dashboard result categories, and no-production-mutation safety boundary.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Add current HEAD, target environment, evidence freshness timestamp, queue liveness status, and CERTIFYING output.
Fail promotion evidence when generated evidence is stale relative to target SHA.
Keep simulated, staging-only, and human-required proof categories distinct.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Promotion pack rejects stale evidence SHA.
Pack emits CERTIFYING:false when unresolved, simulated-only, stale, or human-required proof remains.
Queue liveness failure prevents certification.
doneCriteria: Freshness and certification state are machine-readable.
Stale docs cannot be mistaken for current certification.
No production mutation or live provider calls occur.
regressionRisks: Existing evidence files may become intentionally failing until regenerated.
Stakeholder docs may need updated references to new fields.

findings.title: Pull Request Regression Guardrail Is Too Narrow For A Compliance-Critical Platform
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Existing fast golden-path PR guard and current package scripts.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Add a separate required risk workflow for contracts, API tests, deterministic ingestion, migration governance, packet PDF cache proof, and static workflow checks.
Keep long soak or measured load runs scheduled/pre-promotion if too expensive for every PR.
Avoid adding secrets or live provider calls.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Static workflow test proves required commands are present.
Static workflow test proves no production mutation or live provider call commands are added.
Existing route-auth contract suite remains referenced.
commandsToRun: pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts tests/unit/pr-regression-workflow.spec.ts tests/contracts/route-auth-classification.spec.ts tests/contracts/route-endpoint-surface.spec.ts
doneCriteria: PR guardrails cover more than the happy path.
Heavy checks are placed in the appropriate PR or pre-promotion lane.
Static tests lock workflow intent.
regressionRisks: CI runtime may increase.
Required workflow naming changes can affect branch protection outside the repo.

findings.title: Admin Correction Finalization Performs Multi-Step Truth Changes Without A Single Transaction
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Correction validation requirements, regulation reference checks, training example payload shape, correction detail response shape, and admin-only workflow.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Wrap finalization status update, training upsert, and audit metadata writes in one transaction where possible.
If training generation must remain asynchronous, write explicit pending/degraded state before completion.
Do not change canonical violation conclusions without test evidence and audit trail.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Failure after status update rolls back finalization.
Training upsert failure leaves correction non-final or degraded, not silently complete.
Existing successful finalization detail remains compatible.
doneCriteria: Finalization truth changes are atomic or visibly degraded.
Existing admin correction flows still pass.
No silent canonical truth change occurs.
regressionRisks: Current helper functions may rely on global db and need transaction-aware injection.
Training upsert compatibility with existing rows must be preserved.

findings.title: Upload UI Communicates Queue State, But Backend Liveness Is Not Guaranteed
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Current upload UI flow, server upload limits, queued/running/retry/dead-letter status language, and consumer-safe wording.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Add backend worker liveness and oldest queued age to status payloads.
Display user-safe remediation/fail-closed language when no worker heartbeat exists.
Avoid legal conclusions or technical jargon in consumer-facing copy.
Keep validation automated through React/Vitest tests.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: UI renders fail-closed support/remediation state when worker liveness is absent.
UI does not tell users to wait indefinitely when oldest queued age exceeds threshold.
Normal queued/running/complete states remain unchanged.
doneCriteria: User-facing upload state reflects backend worker liveness.
Copy stays consumer-safe and non-legal-conclusive.
Automated UI tests cover all new states.
regressionRisks: Extra status fields must remain backward-compatible.
Fail-closed copy can increase support contacts if thresholds are too aggressive.

findings.title: Frontend Bundle Size Is Large And Not A Hard Gate
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Current Vite build, route behavior, runtime-size reporting, and warning-only policy unless a later audited task enables a hard gate.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Add or refine bundle reporting for admin/parser/PDF-heavy chunks.
Introduce route-level code splitting incrementally without changing routing semantics.
Keep thresholds warning-only until automated runtime evidence justifies a hard gate.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Runtime-size report captures chunk names and sizes.
Policy reports warning-only status as non-certifying.
Build succeeds after code splitting.
doneCriteria: Bundle risk is measured with current evidence.
Any splitting is route-compatible and covered by build/tests.
Performance claim remains non-certifying until hard gate evidence exists.
regressionRisks: Lazy loading can break admin/parser/PDF routes.
Chunking can hide rather than reduce total shipped code.

findings.title: Packet List Query Uses Client-Side Filtering For Ownership/Organization Context
findings.subsystemToPreserve: Existing packet ownership checks, packet list response shape, pagination behavior, and client-side defensive filtering as display protection only.
smallestSafeImplementationPath: Prove packet list APIs enforce user/org scope server-side.
Add or tighten server-side pagination while retaining current client filtering as a secondary guard.
Avoid changing packet generation, packet PDF, or evidence behavior.
automatedSimulationTestsToAdd: Non-owner cannot receive another user's packet in list results.
Organization-scoped listing returns only authorized packets.
Client filtering remains present but is not the authorization boundary.
commandsToRun: pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts tests/unit/packet-query-scope.spec.ts tests/api/packet-lifecycle-endpoint.spec.ts tests/api/support-role-privacy-matrix.spec.ts
doneCriteria: Packet list scope is enforced server-side with tests.
Client filtering remains defensive and documented.
Packet list performance does not overfetch beyond configured bounds.
regressionRisks: Existing organization/admin list workflows may rely on broader query behavior.
Pagination changes can affect UI expectations.

implementationOrder: Close P0 evidence ledger and artifact durability blockers first.
Close P1 request-bound ingest, queue liveness, persistence atomicity, compliance atomicity, parser gate, packet cache-miss, and rollback/deploy blockers before any broader production claim.
Close P2 governance and auditability gaps with static workflow tests and machine-readable evidence.
Treat P3 items as performance/scale hardening after P0/P1 blockers are automated.
